channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Ubisoft Is Having A Pretty Enormous Sale Right Now
item.content:encoded:
It’s kind of hard to see the massive discounts on Ubisoft games on Steam this week and not think: fire sale! The company’s stock price took a head-first dive in January after its latest botched attempt at “restructuring,” and incredibly it’s yet to show any signs of recovery. Share prices today are the same as they were on the day of that January 22 crash, leaving the company worth less than a twentieth of its value in 2018. Things are bad. So presumably there’s hope that knocking half the price off of last year’s Assassin’s Creed Shadows might at least keep the lights on.

It’s actually a fantastic sale for us. Games less than two years old are up to 90 percent off, and top-notch classics have similar discounts off their already far lower prices. Because for as badly run as Ubisoft appears to have been for many years, the publisher puts out some genuinely excellent games. And a game as interesting as the “crappy masterpiece” Star Wars Outlaws (in its much-improved updated version) being just $21 is something to take advantage of. Here are a few suggestions of games to grab while they’re cheap:

Assassin’s Creed Shadows – $35

Last year’s entry into the long-running Assassin’s Creed series was good at launch, has been updated since release, and reached fine fettle by September 2025. You play as both a stealthy shinobi assassin and a formidable samurai, fusing parkour goodness, stealthy fighting, and more overt action in one of the most spectacularly pretty games ever made. If your machine can cope. It had another raft of improvements last fall, and right now is 50 percent off.

Far Cry Primal – $3

I love Far Cry Primal. The Far Cry series has been hit-or-miss, the latter mostly due to reheated ideas. But Primal took the format somewhere new…well, old, with the action set 10,000 years in the past and the guns replaced with pointy sticks but keeping all the same base-raiding, animal-hunting, skin-peeling antics that makes the series fun. It came out in 2016 and still looks extremely pretty, and at 90 percent off is available for the comically low price of three bucks.

Tom Clancy’s The Division 2 – $3

Jokes about Tom Clancy still making games 13 years after his death are now so old as to have died themselves, but still, the poor man’s name is attached to so many Ubisoft games. “Grandaddy, what is a Tom Clancy?” ask today’s young players, and are told to hush because I’m trying to snipe the bad man’s head off. The Division 2 has had a bit of a tumultuous ride for those playing it as a live-service game, losing a lot of fans over the years since its original 2019 release, but if you started playing today for just $3 you’re in for a really decent core game.

Avatar: Frontiers of Pandora – $30

I would never have thought to play the movie tie-in game of the Avatar movie franchise, if it weren’t for Zack’s passionate campaigning for the game to get the attention he says it deserves. It’s 35 percent off during this sale, making it a much more reasonable $30 if you’re willing to trust the man. Although it’s also just been added to Game Pass, if you’re looking to pay even less.

Star Wars Outlaws – $21

Outlaws had a rough ride on release. That was partly because it launched with far too many bugs, broken stealth, and enemy AI that really leaned hard on the “A”, but also partly because of the worst people on the internet putting Musk-endorsed effort into destroying it because the game had the temerity to feature a woman as its main character. But ignore those knuckle-dragging knob-heads, because it remains a fantastic experience, especially now that so many of the bugs have been ironed out, and the stealth much improved. It’s 70 percent off, which is a significant chunk for a game only 16 months old.



https://kotaku.com/ubisoft-steam-sale-assassins-creed-shadows-star-wars-2000675127
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sims 4 Players Don’t Understand How ‘Disgusting’ Epstein Family Made It Into Game’s Gallery
item.content:encoded:
A Sims 4 player has created and uploaded a Jeffrey Epstein family to the game’s online content gallery. The family features multiple children wearing only towels. It is one of many Epstein-related creations shared on The Sims 4‘s gallery, some of which have been around for months or even years.

In January, more of convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein’s files and emails were released to the public. This has once again kicked up interest online in the now-dead criminal sex offender and his island, where allegedly many rich and powerful people were invited to partake in parties allegedly involving underage children. And at least one person, upon seeing all this back in the news, hopped onto The Sims 4 to create a digital “family” featuring Epstein and seven partially nude children.

As spotted by a poster on the Sims 4 subreddit on March 2, a new user uploaded the family “NPC Children Townies” to the game’s free user gallery. This is a place where players can share user-created builds and characters with other players for free. While the “NPC Children Townies” title doesn’t directly name Epstein, it uses a model heavily inspired by the real person, and which appears to be based on (or directly ripped from) other Epstein characters available on the gallery. Because yeah, there are a few of them floating around The Sims 4 and its user gallery.

On the Sims 4 subreddit, players were upset by the new Epstein-themed family featuring half-naked kids. “This is absolutely despicable,” said one user. “This is just disgusting,” said another. Plenty of users also complained that EA’s moderation of creations in the past has been overly strict, yet these Epstein creations are able to make it onto the gallery and stick around.

Kotaku was able to confirm in-game that there are multiple Epstein characters available to download. Some of these are explicitly named Jeffrey Epstein or some variation on that. Some of these are from earlier this year. One of the most popular features both Epstein and one of his associates, convicted sex offender Ghislaine Maxwell.

Simply titled “Epstein,” this duo was uploaded to the gallery back in 2023, and over 1,000 users have downloaded the criminal pair. The description for the created duo simply reads “Great with kids.” Both the “NPC Children Townies” family and the “Epstein” duo were still available to download as of March 2 at 4:20 p.m. EST.

Kotaku has contacted EA about the Epstein creations on the Sims 4 gallery.

Update: 3/2/2026: 5:15 p.m. EST: An EA spokesperson told Kotaku that this content is “wholly unacceptable, and our team has removed it.”

“If we detect content that violates our User Agreement and Positive Play Charter, we may take a number of enforcement actions, including removing the content and the player’s access to certain or all EA Services. We encourage players to report this kind of content in The Sims Gallery by clicking Flag This on the item.”

Kotaku checked, and the “Townies” family has now been removed from the Sims 4 gallery. However, the other Epstein creations remain. Kotaku has reached out to EA about these other user-made characters.



https://kotaku.com/sims-4-jeffrey-epstein-kids-family-ea-user-generated-content-gallery-fans-angry-2000675121
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
